# {{TITLE}}

See LAP-2154 (canonical BO) release-note style: "When 'Initiate E-Mandate Registration?' is set to Yes on Repayment Details, the system either shows a fresh link or skips to success based on existing mandate validity." Trigger + outcome. -->

## Intent

{{INTENT_PARAGRAPH}}
<!-- TIP: 1 paragraph. State what + why. BO intent must contain "if X then ... if Y then ..." structure or equivalent — one operator action, ≥2 outcomes. See LAP-2154 (canonical BO) opening paragraph — names the screen, the dropdown, the branching logic in 4 lines. LAP-2046 §1 "Problem Statement" is a useful secondary reference for framing the policy gap that necessitates the branch (note: LAP-2046 is type WC, not BO, but its Step-2 internal Cases are structurally similar). -->

## Problem

{{PROBLEM_PARAGRAPH}}
<!-- TIP: state the operational pain — what goes wrong today when the system doesn't branch correctly. LAP-2154 implies its problem (link is sent unnecessarily when a valid mandate already exists). LAP-2046 §1 names a similar pain explicitly (data-mismatch issue: PAN DOB) — useful prose model when the operational pain is concrete enough to name. -->

## Current flow that we want to fix

1. {{CURRENT_STEP_1}}
2. {{CURRENT_STEP_2}}
3. {{CURRENT_STEP_3}}
<!-- TIP: numbered. Show the un-branched state today and where it fails. Skip this section ONLY if the trigger action is brand-new (rare for BO). -->

## Proposed solution

### Step 1: {{TRIGGER_OR_CHECK}}

{{STEP_1_PARAGRAPH}}
<!-- TIP: the system-side check that determines which branch fires. See LAP-2154 conditions block ("Sanction Loan amount ≤ Mandate amount" AND "Last EMI date + 5 years ≤ mandate_expiry_date") — names the exact backend conditions. LAP-2046 §"Step 1: Check VCIP Status" is a simpler one-paragraph variant when only one input is checked. -->

### Step 2: {{THE_ONE_OPERATOR_ACTION_THAT_BRANCHES}}

{{LEAD_IN_PARAGRAPH_TO_CASES}}
<!-- TIP: name the single operator click / submit / decision that triggers the fork. The cases follow. Per BO discipline: ONE action, N branches. If the operator does two different things, this is two BO tickets. -->

#### Case 1: {{BRANCH_CONDITION_1}}

- **What the operator sees:** '{{SYSTEM_STRING_OR_SCREEN}}'
- **What the operator does:** {{ACTION}}
- **What the system does silently:** {{SILENT_SYSTEM_ACTION}}
- **How the operator verifies (if silent):** {{VERIFICATION_METHOD}}
- **Auto-comm fired:** {{SMS_OR_EMAIL_OR_NONE}}
- **Consequence:** {{DOWNSTREAM_OUTCOME}}
<!-- TIP: the See/Do/Verify triplet is the BO contract. See LAP-2154 (canonical BO) "If the mandate is already approved and conditions are met" branch — operator sees success callback (no link), system silently pushes NACH reference to LMS, operator verifies via the latest NACH Reference number in the push-to-LMS request. LAP-2046 §"Case 1: VCIP Done = YES" is a simpler reference when the verification is just "validity assumed". Auto-comm flag is mandatory: if SMS/email/notification fires silently, name it verbatim. If nothing fires, write "None". -->

#### Case 2: {{BRANCH_CONDITION_2}}

- **What the operator sees:** '{{SYSTEM_STRING_OR_SCREEN}}'
- **What the operator does:** {{ACTION}}
- **What the system does silently:** {{SILENT_SYSTEM_ACTION}}
- **How the operator verifies (if silent):** {{VERIFICATION_METHOD}}
- **Auto-comm fired:** {{SMS_OR_EMAIL_OR_NONE}}
- **Consequence:** {{DOWNSTREAM_OUTCOME}}
- **Non-overridable rules:** {{HARD_GUARDRAILS_OPERATOR_CANNOT_BYPASS}}
<!-- TIP: non-overridable rules go here when the panel has no edit option. Phrase as "you cannot change this from the panel" — operators read these and stop trying workarounds. -->

#### Case 3: {{BRANCH_CONDITION_3}}

- **What the operator sees:** '{{SYSTEM_STRING_OR_SCREEN}}'
- **What the operator does:** {{ACTION}}
- **What the system does silently:** {{SILENT_SYSTEM_ACTION}}
- **Consequence:** {{DOWNSTREAM_OUTCOME}}

## Applicability

{{APPLICABILITY_NARRATIVE_OR_LIST}}
<!-- TIP: mandatory for BO. Who / what loan types / what user states the branch applies to. LAP-2154 doesn't carry a separate Applicability section (the Repayment Details subtab is the implicit scope). For tickets where applicability needs to be spelled out, see LAP-2046 §3 "Applicability" — Applicants × User Scenarios × Loan Types as a 3-list grid. -->

## Exception

{{EXCEPTION_PARAGRAPH}}
<!-- TIP: optional. ONE case that is a real exception (not a branch — a carve-out). See LAP-2154 §"Edge case — buffer policy" (revised mandate uses 4-year buffer instead of 5; new mandate generated via link goes back to 5-year buffer). LAP-2046 §4 "Exception" is a simpler population-exemption variant (Internal Top-Up never triggers PAN re-verification regardless of VCIP). Use this section when one slice of the population OR one downstream sub-case is exempt from all branches. -->
